scenario_config.name: before_all_optimizations
_comparison_notes: The cumulative headline comparison for the thesis (per
shape, but every shipped optimization (A, B, D, F) is reverted.
F (Redis write buffer + flusher) -> baseline/pre-write-buffer image +
Because the F baseline image predates C (rollup-only analytics) and E
(django-ninja ingest) as well, this comparison technically also strips
those two. That is acceptable for the headline chart: the thesis only
claims A+B+D+F as the bundled wins; C and E are documented separately.
Verify scenario_summary.stack_state after the run to confirm every flip
actually landed (PG defaults, dummy cache, no PgBouncer, no flusher,
Expected: large multi-axis regression vs national_medium — much higher
p95, many more drops, lower sustained RPS, higher backend CPU.
_dataset_notes: Copied verbatim from national_medium.json so the pair is dataset-identical.
Cross-check via dataset_report.weeks in the generated reports.
_traffic_notes: Traffic block copied verbatim from national_medium.json. The target rates
are likely UNREACHABLE on the reverted stack; that is the point. Drops and
tail latency are the headline numbers, not throughput parity.
VUs are sized as in national_medium so any drops come from server

load_health.note: Server over capacity: 19.1% of iterations dropped, 0.0% of checks failed. Latency numbers represent only the requests that got through and are not representative of what the server would do at realistic load. Reduce rates significantly before drawing conclusions.
